title: Mexico City is sinking up to 14 inches per year, satellite images show
summary: Satellite images reveal Mexico City is sinking at a rate of up to 14 inches per year, making it one of the fastest subsiding cities globally.

title: NASA just released 12,000 photos from Artemis 2. Here are our top picks
summary: NASA released 12,000 photos from the Artemis 2 moon mission. The images provide a detailed look at the mission's progress and activities.

title: Watch SpaceX Starlink train circle Earth in amazing satellite video
summary: A newly launched SpaceX Starlink satellite captured a video showing its view of Earth as it orbits the planet. The footage highlights the visual experience of circling above Earth.

title: Moon lander preps for the lunar surface | Space photo of the day for May 6, 2026
summary: Blue Origin's MK1 moon lander has successfully completed vacuum chamber tests. The development is part of NASA's efforts to prepare for astronauts' return to the moon.

title: 'SOL Shogunate' is a samurai space opera set on the moon, but with an aerospace engineer making sure it's 'getting the science right'
summary: The space opera 'SOL Shogunate' is set on the moon and aims to blend science fiction with accurate lunar science. Aerospace engineer Taylore McClurg and Chaos Manufacturing CEO Guy Costantini discuss the creative process behind the believable lunar adventure.

title: These 2 companies want to start removing space junk from orbit in 2027
summary: Two private companies are collaborating to launch a space debris removal service for low Earth orbit in 2027. This initiative aims to establish a repeatable and sustainable solution for cleaning up orbital debris.

title: Forget the AT-ST, at 20% off this Lego Star Wars AT-TE is the king of Star Wars walkers
summary: A Lego Star Wars AT-TE walker set is currently on sale for 20% off, offering a detailed Clone Wars-era vehicle with extra features. The promotion highlights the durability and collectible nature of the Lego set.

title: Former NASA chief Jim Bridenstine takes over as CEO of Quantum Space
summary: Former NASA Administrator Jim Bridenstine has been appointed as CEO of Quantum Space, a company focused on developing maneuverable spacecraft for military and commercial applications.

title: NASA is making a powerful new ion engine to send astronauts to Mars — and it just passed its 1st test
summary: NASA has developed a new ion engine using lithium metal vapor as propellant, achieving 25 times more power than its Psyche mission counterpart. The prototype passed its first tests, paving the way for potential Mars missions.

title: NASA eClips and GLOBE Educators Strengthen a Regional STEM Ecosystem in Coastal Virginia
summary: NASA eClips and GLOBE educators partnered with Virginia school districts and community groups to enhance STEM education in the Tidewater region. The collaboration aims to strengthen regional STEM ecosystems through instructional practice and environmental learning.

title: NASA’s Perseverance Mars Rover Surveys ‘Crocodile Bridge’
summary: NASA's Perseverance Mars rover captured a 360-degree panorama of a region called 'Crocodile Bridge' on Jezero Crater's rim using its Mastcam-Z camera system. The image is composed of 980 images taken over two days in December 2025.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4879-4885: Struggle at Atacama
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover faces challenges while exploring Chile's Atacama Desert, one of the driest places on Earth. The mission team details the rover's activities and struggles in this extreme environment.

title: 650 NASA Volunteers Have Co-Authored Scientific Papers
summary: NASA Citizen Science reports that over 650 volunteers have co-authored peer-reviewed research papers. These volunteers contributed significantly to scientific projects alongside NASA scientists.

title: NASA’s Curiosity Rover Frees Its Drill From a Rock
summary: NASA's Curiosity Rover successfully freed its drill after a rock became stuck to it during a drilling operation on Mars. The process was captured by the rover's hazard cameras.

title: NASA Astronaut to Answer Questions from Students in Florida
summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams will answer STEM questions from Florida students during a live call from the International Space Station on May 8. The event will be streamed on NASA's YouTube channel.

title: NASA Research Shows Early Life Relied on Rare Metal
summary: NASA-funded research reveals that life on Earth over 3 billion years ago depended on the rare metal molybdenum, which was scarce at the time. The study, published in Nature Communications, is the first to demonstrate molybdenum's role in ancient life.

title: Building on America’s 65-Year Legacy of Human Spaceflight
summary: On May 5, 1961, the Mercury-Redstone 3 mission launched Alan B. Shepard Jr. into space, marking the first American human spaceflight. The 15-minute suborbital flight from Cape Canaveral established a 65-year legacy of human spaceflight in the U.S.

title: NASA’S Juno Misson Captures Jupiter Moon Thebe
summary: NASA's Juno spacecraft captured an image of Jupiter's moon Thebe during a close pass on May 1, 2026. The Stellar Reference Unit (SRU) took the photo from 3,100 miles away with a resolution of 1.9 miles per pixel.

title: New NASA HEAT Coloring Book Blends Art, Science, and Cultural Perspectives
summary: NASA, in partnership with the University of Alaska Fairbanks, has released a new Sun-centered coloring book that combines art, science, and cultural perspectives. The HEAT coloring book aims to educate while encouraging creativity.

title: Pad 1 conversion ongoing, with Flight 12 nearing
summary: Pad 1 is undergoing conversion as Flight 12 approaches, six months after the completion of Flight 11. The old design of Pad 1 is being upgraded for future missions.

title: Launch Preview: three launches — a calm before the storm?
summary: Three launches are scheduled, including two SpaceX Falcon 9 missions and one Chang Zheng 7 mission. This may precede a busier launch period.

title: SpaceX launches CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its third rideshare mission of 2026 aboard a Falcon 9 rocket, carrying CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to a Sun-synchronous orbit.

title: SpaceX aims for mid-May Starship Flight 12 launch with revised trajectory
summary: SpaceX is targeting a mid-May launch for its 12th Starship flight test with a revised trajectory. The company has issued notices for the upcoming launch attempt.

title: Russia debuts new rocket with maiden Soyuz-5 launch
summary: Russia successfully launched its new Soyuz-5 rocket on its maiden flight from the Baikonur Cosmodrome. The debut marks a significant milestone for the country's space program.

title: Next NASA ISS astronaut, doctor discusses upcoming mission
summary: A NASA astronaut and doctor discusses their upcoming mission to the International Space Station (ISS). The astronaut has experience in mass casualty events and military service overseas.

title: SpaceX launches Falcon Heavy on Wednesday after 18 month hiatus
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its Falcon Heavy rocket after an 18-month hiatus. This marks a significant return for the heavy-lift vehicle.

title: Artemis III core stage assembly arrives at the Kennedy Space Center
summary: The core stage assembly for NASA's Artemis III mission has arrived at the Kennedy Space Center. This follows the historic Artemis II launch, which sent four astronauts on a lunar flyby mission.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon Heavy returns, Atlas V and Ariane 6 to launch Amazon Leo satellites
summary: SpaceX's Falcon Heavy, ULA's Atlas V, and Ariane 6 are set to launch Amazon's Leo satellites this week from multiple locations. Six launches are planned across Florida, California, and French Guiana.

title: Curiosity Rover uncovers fresh organic clues on Ancient Mars
summary: NASA's Curiosity Rover has discovered new organic compounds on Mars, providing further clues about the potential for ancient life on the Red Planet. The findings mark a significant advancement in the search for extraterrestrial life.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years of achievements with a commemorative booklet highlighting 50 hallmark milestones. The publication reflects on five decades of contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space mission involving a parachute test underwent a 'bake-out' procedure to ensure its reliability. The process likely involved heating the parachute to remove moisture or contaminants.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: The ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is free of Earth microbes before landing on Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover during its descent to the Red Planet, where it will search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for space applications, offering a novel method for capturing and manipulating objects in orbit. This innovation could significantly enhance satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual consultation process through the SME Forum 2026, held in Paris on June 8-9, 2026. The event includes an online preparatory forum in April and May.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal was successfully transmitted from an optimal location. The achievement marks a significant milestone for the space navigation technology.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has conducted 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data. The mission revealed that solar wind structures in the inner corona move 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Commerce Aiming to Open Novel Space Applications This Summer
summary: The Office of Space Commerce (OSC) plans to streamline licensing for novel space missions by this summer. This initiative aims to simplify the regulatory process for emerging space applications.

title: Bridenstine Takes Over as Quantum Space CEO
summary: Jim Bridenstine, former NASA administrator, has been appointed as CEO of Quantum Space. He expressed enthusiasm for returning to national security and defense work, aligning with his background.

title: Anduril Names Its SBI Team For Golden Dome
summary: Anduril has named its team for the Space-Based Interceptor (SBI) component of the Golden Dome missile defense architecture. The company is one of 12 prime contractors involved in this project.

title: ESA Taps Edge Aerospace for Space Cloud Contract
summary: ESA has selected Edge Aerospace, a Luxembourg-based company, to develop an architecture and use-case roadmap for orbital data centers under a new space cloud contract.

title: Lt. Gen. Schiess Nominated to Lead Space Force
summary: Lt. Gen. Schiess has been nominated to lead the U.S. Space Force. If confirmed, he will become the third chief of space operations since the Space Force's establishment in 2019.

title: Payload Field Guide: Lunar Rovers
summary: NASA and its partners are advancing lunar rovers to support sustained human presence on the Moon, moving beyond Apollo-era designs to more capable vehicles for long-term surface operations.

title: Space Nuclear Execs Cheer the FY27 Budget Proposal
summary: NASA's FY27 budget proposal has been praised by space nuclear executives, with Administrator Isaacman highlighting it as a logical evolution for the agency. The proposal signals strong agency support beyond just funding.

title: House Subcommittee Approves $24.4B NASA Budget
summary: The House Subcommittee has approved a $24.4 billion budget for NASA, rejecting the administration's FY27 budget request. This marks the first legislative step to formally oppose the administration's proposed spending plan.

title: European Space Firms Raised €1.4B of Private Capital in 2025
summary: European space firms raised €1.4 billion in private capital in 2025, reflecting growing investor confidence in the sector. This trend highlights the expanding role of private markets in funding space industry innovation.

title: BioOrbit Raises £9.8M Seed To Manufacture Cancer Drugs in Orbit
summary: BioOrbit has secured £9.8 million in seed funding to produce cancer drugs in orbit using microgravity, aiming to enable self-administration by patients. The company leverages space-based manufacturing to reformulate drugs for easier patient use.

title: SWISSto12 Strikes German Partnership Deal for D2D GEO Satellite
summary: SWISSto12 has formed a new partnership in Germany to enhance media broadcast capabilities for Geostationary (GEO) satellites. The collaboration focuses on high-performance space subsystems.

title: Anduril to Work With Impulse Space, K2 Space, Voyager, and Others on Space-Based Interceptor Award
summary: Anduril Industries has partnered with Impulse Space, Inversion Space, K2 Space, and others to prototype space-based interceptor technologies. The collaboration follows Anduril's recent award to develop these systems.

title: Telesat’s GEO Business Declines 25% in Q1
summary: Telesat's GEO business revenue dropped by 25% year-over-year in Q1 2026. The decline is attributed to the company's shift toward launching its Lightspeed constellation.

title: What Amazon’s Globalstar Acquisition Means for MSS Spectrum and D2D Market: Analyst Roundtable
summary: Amazon acquired Globalstar in a $10.8 billion deal, signaling its expansion into mobile satellite services (MSS) and direct-to-device (D2D) communications. The acquisition could reshape spectrum dynamics and competition in the satellite industry.

title: NRO Makes New Commercial Awards to EarthDaily, Iceye, and Pixxel
summary: The National Reconnaissance Office (NRO) awarded new contracts for Earth sensing data to EarthDaily, Iceye, and Pixxel under its Strategic Commercial Enhancements (SCE) effort. This move expands the NRO's commercial satellite data partnerships.

title: STMicroelectronics Expects its Space Business to Reach More than $3B Over Three Years
summary: STMicroelectronics, a global semiconductor company, expects its space business to exceed $3 billion in revenue over the next three years. The company supplies components to SpaceX and other space industry players.

title: Lockheed Martin Joins Seagate Space and Firefly Aerospace to Develop Sea-Based Launch
summary: Lockheed Martin has joined Seagate Space and Firefly Aerospace to collaborate on developing a sea-based launch platform. The partnership aims to advance offshore launch capabilities for space missions.

title: Future-Proofing Satellite Networks With ST Engineering iDirect Sales SVP Brian Jakins
summary: ST Engineering iDirect's Sales SVP Brian Jakins discusses strategies to future-proof satellite networks amid advancements like 5G. The article highlights the challenges and solutions for network operators in an increasingly connected world.

title: Vantor Nabs $70 Million NGA Award For GEOINT Portal Use
summary: Vantor secured a $70 million award from the National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency (NGA) for the first option year of a GEOINT portal program. The contract supports the agency and vetted partners in geospatial intelligence operations.

title: Lynk, EarthDaily, True Anomaly Launch Satellites on Weekend SpaceX Rideshare Mission
summary: SpaceX launched 45 payloads, including satellites from Lynk, EarthDaily, and True Anomaly, on a rideshare mission to deploy a South Korean Earth Observation satellite. The mission highlights the growing role of commercial spaceflight in deploying multiple payloads simultaneously.
